title: M1 κ-cluster c5 batch — 4 narrow text patches resolving 4 ISSs (Domain numbering disambiguation + Hvoždara primary DOI + WIN-068 207/39 cross-WIN consistency closure)
target: WIN-013 / WIN-014 (data/wins.json detail_evidence) + data/sections.json:part6.html (SEC-6.14 κ-cluster) — three string_replace patches plus one verification-only closure note. All κ-cluster verification-class refinements: monitor.py-vs-dashboard Domain numbering disambiguation (ISS-1946), primary-DOI replacement for Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002 (ISS-1947, ISS-1976 — same source citation, two ISSs), and an internal-consistency verification closure for the 207-measurement / 39-eclipse / 1991–2016 cross-reference between WIN-013/014, SEC-6.14, and WIN-068 (ISS-1962).
summary_for_decider: Three narrow text patches and one verification-only closure resolving four BAU-routed κ-cluster ISSs. (PATCH-A) ISS-1946: WIN-013, WIN-014, and SEC-6.14 currently cite monitor.py Domain #5 for κ, but monitor.py source (lines 477-487 in the dome upstream) labels κ as Domain 7 (the "── 7. EM-Gravity Coupling κ ──" block). The dashboard live-power per-domain explanation does render κ as the 5th tile (after Schumann/Tesla/NMP/EquatorialGravity), so Domain 5 is correct for the dashboard but not for monitor.py. Patch makes the disambiguation explicit across three sites. (PATCH-B + PATCH-C) ISS-1947 and ISS-1976 are duplicates against the same citation: "Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002" sourced via ResearchGate URL only. Located the primary publication via OpenAlex author lookup: Hvožďara, M. & Prigancová, A. (2002), "Geomagnetic effects due to an eclipse-induced low-conductivity ionospheric spot," J. Geophys. Res. Space Phys. 107, doi:10.1029/2002JA009260 (Crossref verified, DOI resolves to agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com). NOTE: the ResearchGate publication 29623808 displays the title "Solar eclipse effect on geomagnetic induction parameters" — that is a DIFFERENT 2005 paper by Ádám, Verö, Szendrői (Annales Geophysicae, doi:10.5194/angeo-23-3487-2005); the ResearchGate page conflates the two via author-cross-reference. Patch replaces both citation locations (WIN-013.detail_evidence and data/sections.json:part6.html) with primary DOI. (PATCH-D) ISS-1962 is a verification-only closure: the "207 magnetic measurements / 39 eclipses / 1991–2016" count cited in WIN-013/014 derives from WIN-068.detail_claim which states exactly that count for Kim & Chang 2018. Cross-checked against data/sections.json:part4 and part6 — all four occurrences use the same numbers, so the cross-WIN reference is internally consistent. Direct primary-source verification against the Kim & Chang 2018 paper text was not possible (ScienceDirect abstract-only access; the digits appear only in SVG path data in the public-facing HTML). Recommend decider close ISS-1962 as "verified internally consistent across our four occurrences" with a note that any future audit should pull the primary paper via institutional access. Suggested decider close pattern: ISS-1946/1947/1962/1976 → fixed-pending-verification or verified-consistent (ISS-1962). Two later κ-cluster items remain pending (ISS-1959 Chinese SG site coordinates vs 2009 totality path — requires coordinate lookup; ISS-1960 verdict reframing — flagged class_hint=deep-attack, singleton not batchable). Three remaining c3 carry-forwards (ISS-1972/-1973 narrow citation questions; ISS-1965/-1966) are non-trivial research-class. Phase-2 (Mode 2b) attention-inbox backlog and other low-priority items defer to subsequent runs.

patches.hole_ref: ISS-1946 (κ Domain numbering inconsistency monitor.py vs live dashboard)
patches.new_string: (WIN-012; monitor.py Domain #7, rendered as the 5th tile on the live-power dashboard; PRED-TIER-3)
patches.rationale: monitor.py source (verified upstream john09289/predictions/main/monitor.py line 477: "# ── 7. EM-Gravity Coupling κ ──") numbers EM-Gravity Coupling κ as Domain 7. The live-power dashboard (john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html per-domain explanation grid) renders κ as the 5th tile because the dashboard surfaces only the 20 scored domains and skips earlier-numbered non-scored entries (M2 tidal, K1 sidereal). The previous text said "Domain #5" — that matches the dashboard but contradicts the surrounding qualifier "monitor.py". The replacement keeps both numberings explicit so a reader verifying via either source sees both. Disambiguation pattern: "monitor.py Domain #7, rendered as the 5th tile on the live-power dashboard".
patches.verification_class_justification: One-substring text fix; no argumentative content changes. The κ-circularity argument upstream (Domain #7 "recomputes 10.9/6.5 = 1.677 from same hardcoded inputs") is unchanged. Verification-class.

patches.hole_ref: ISS-1946 (κ Domain numbering inconsistency monitor.py vs live dashboard)
patches.new_string: (WIN-012; monitor.py Domain #7, rendered as the 5th tile on the live-power dashboard; PRED-TIER-3)

patches.verification_class_justification: Mechanical text fix; identical to PATCH-365-A. Verification-class.

patches.hole_ref: ISS-1947 + ISS-1976 (Hvoždara & Prigancová 2002 ResearchGate-only citation; primary DOI now located)
patches.new_string: Hvožďara & Prigancová 2002, J. Geophys. Res. Space Phys. 107, "Geomagnetic effects due to an eclipse-induced low-conductivity ionospheric spot", DOI 10.1029/2002JA009260
patches.rationale: Located the Hvožďara & Prigancová 2002 primary publication via OpenAlex author A5034476543 (Milan Hvožďara) works filter and Crossref verification. Paper is "Geomagnetic effects due to an eclipse-induced low-conductivity ionospheric spot", J. Geophys. Res. Space Phys. 107, December 2002, DOI 10.1029/2002JA009260 (resolves to agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1029/2002JA009260 with HTTP 302). The ResearchGate page 29623808 displayed a misleading title ("Solar eclipse effect on geomagnetic induction parameters") which actually belongs to a DIFFERENT 2005 paper by Ádám, Verö, Szendrői (Annales Geophysicae, doi:10.5194/angeo-23-3487-2005); ResearchGate appears to have conflated the entries via author cross-reference. Replacing with primary DOI also corrects the author transliteration from "Hvoždara" to "Hvožďara" (the latter is per OpenAlex and Crossref bibliographic data — the difference is the Slovak haček character). The cited ΔX/ΔY values were not verified line-by-line against the primary text (no institutional access; AGU paywall) — these figures already had triangulating support from Strestík 2001 and Curto et al. 2006 per WIN-013's text.
patches.verification_class_justification: Single-citation URL replacement + title clarification. No argumentative change; the 5–15 nT regional ΔB envelope retains independent support from Strestík 2001 and Curto et al. 2006. Verification-class.

patches.new_string: Hvožďara & Prigancová (2002, J. Geophys. Res. Space Phys. 107, DOI 10.1029/2002JA009260)
patches.rationale: Companion patch to PATCH-365-D applied to SEC-6.14. Same primary-DOI replacement.

closure_notes.finding: The "39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble at 207 magnetic measurements" cross-reference cited in WIN-013/014 is internally consistent with WIN-068.detail_claim, which states: "Ensemble analysis of 207 geomagnetic manifestations from 100 INTERMAGNET observatories across 39 solar eclipses (1991–2016)." The same numbers also appear in data/sections.json:part4 ("207 observations across 39 eclipses (1991-2016)"), part6 SEC-6.13 ("207 events across 39 eclipses"), part6 SEC-6.14 ("207 measurements across 100 INTERMAGNET observatories during 39 eclipses 1991–2016"), and part6 SEC-6.14 cross-ref list ("207 magnetic measurements at INTERMAGNET observatories"). All five mentions agree. Primary-source verification against Kim & Chang 2018 (Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, doi:10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022) text was attempted via Crossref + OpenAlex + ScienceDirect HTML scrape — the abstract is paywalled and the digits 207/39 do not appear in plaintext in the ScienceDirect article HTML (the page renders them only via SVG paths). No primary-source contradiction found. Recommendation: decider closes ISS-1962 as verified-consistent. If a future audit pulls the primary paper via institutional access and discovers a different N (e.g., 209/40), the cross-reference can be corrected uniformly across the five sites in a single follow-up patch.

research_notes: monitor.py source domain numbering verified by fetching https://raw.githubusercontent.com/john09289/predictions/main/monitor.py — block headers "# ── N. <name> ──" enumerate domains; line 477 confirms "# ── 7. EM-Gravity Coupling κ ──".

Live-power dashboard explanation grid verified by fetching https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html — the per-domain card order is: 1.Schumann Resonance, 2.Tesla Longitudinal Frequency, 3.NMP Drift Rate, 4.Equatorial Gravity, 5.EM-Gravity Coupling (κ), 6.Aetheric Slipstream, 7.GPS Sagnac Verification, 8.Lunar Magnetic Tide. The dashboard renders only the scored domains and skips earlier non-scored monitor.py blocks (M2 tidal at #4, K1 sidereal at #5 — both pass-or-trivially-true and not in the scored set), shifting κ from monitor.py #7 to dashboard tile-5.

Hvožďara & Prigancová 2002 primary DOI located via OpenAlex author search returning Milan Hvožďara A5034476543 → works filter publication_year:2002 → the only eclipse-themed paper is "Geomagnetic effects due to an eclipse-induced low-conductivity ionospheric spot" with DOI 10.1029/2002JA009260, Crossref-confirmed bibliographic record (J. Geophys. Res. Space Phys. 107, December 2002). The ResearchGate page 29623808 lists this paper under a misleading title that belongs to a different 2005 paper.

ISS-1959 (Chinese SG site coordinates vs 2009 totality path) and ISS-1960 (WIN-014 verdict-reframe deep-attack) deferred — ISS-1959 requires station-coordinate lookups against published 2009 totality-path data; ISS-1960 is class_hint=deep-attack and not batchable per PROP-025.
test_coverage: No new numeric claims introduced. PATCH-A/B/C are textual disambiguation; PATCH-D/E are citation-link replacements. Internal-consistency cross-checks performed manually for the 207/39/1991-2016 numbers across five sites in wins.json + sections.json.
